column8: details
freed from prison, stole a horse, free peasant dinner, a new adventure awaits
freed from prison, given a horse and some gold by the bemused king
froze to death
anticlimactically sent back to your own time
return home to family and friends
warped into a war zone after asking for guaranteed safe return
anticlimactically sent back to your own time
traveling through the tundra with the hunter/gatherers, you survive but will never return to your own time
returned to your own era, but 11 years have passed
after surviving an unintentional mammoth ride, you are accepted into a paleolithic tribe
thrown from a mammoth and die but create fascinatingly anachronistic remains in the process

your utopian future existence consists of access to the internet, an archive of 300+ year-old films, and interactive, CYOA-inspired video-on-demand that makes the real world too boring to bother exploring
you grown bored of utopia and escape to another time where you are promptly  devoured by sheep-sized cicadas
you joyfully discover surfing in what is either the distant future or past
strangled by a boa constrictor in your sleep
you escape the Titanic in a life boat but will never return to your own time
a paleontologist breaks into your house and steals the dinosaur egg you brought back
you become a fisherman on loch ness until one day the monster devours you
you meet a girl from the post-car-culture America of 2022, but then the two of you are whisked back to the Reagan era
you grow bored of your small fishing village and head for London but are captured by vassals of a nearby lord and hanged
you meet a girl from the near future and attempt to lead her back to your time but overshoot by 4 billion years
after proving your Stone Age mettle by slaying a wolf, you fall into a ravine and awake back in your own time to learn you merely hit your head and had been unconscious
you are conscripted into forced labor building the Great Wall of China
stumble into a saber-toothed tiger’s den
you are accepted by the tribe but awake the next day in your own time—was it all a dream?
you flee the Titanic and are trapped in a bomb shelter during The Blitz
marooned on a reef in an endless ocean
you return to the precise instant you departed from but richer for your experience
you flee a triceratops and meet your end after bumping into a T. Rex
you meet Abe Lincoln and are taken in by a kind farming family near Gettysberg
you live out your life in the past as a carpenter, journalist, and world traveller and die shortly before you were born
you bid Lincoln farewell before Gettysberg and follow the family that adopts you westward to California
the tunnel leads to a pre-historic Earth that has not yet formed an atmosphere and you suffocate
rather than live forever in the timeless space of the cave you return to your loved ones (just in time for dinner)
you give the dinosaur egg to a zoologist and eagerly await news of its hatching
you spend your life at sea, eventually captaining your own tall ship
you become a printer and publish an 18th century newspaper
you save your tubercular friend by leading him back to your own time (and the wonders of its medical system)
the judge dismisses the case against you and takes you in
you fall asleep in the spacecraft but awaken back in the cave. when you revisit it years later you are quietly relieved to discover it has been sealed
